The graphic bore the text “NMPW,” referring to the FB group New Malacañang Pro-Wrestling, further indicating its source. The FB group posts politics and wrestling-related memes.
While some netizens took it as a joke, others who were unfamiliar with the wrestler believed the statement to be true. Some netizens even insinuated that Estrada and Hernandez must have had a romantic relationship.
The fake quote card circulated more than a week after Hernandez implicated Estrada in the flood control controversy during a Sept. 9 House hearing. Hernandez alleged that Estrada and Sen. Joel Villanueva received 30% kickbacks from the flood control projects, which the senators have vehemently denied.
Estrada confronted Hernandez during the Sept. 18 Senate Blue Ribbon Committee about the allegation. Hernandez was not able to show proof that Estrada had a Senate staff named “Beng Ramos,” who was supposedly in contact with a contractor who delivered “obligations” or money to the senator. Estrada maintained that Ramos was not his staff. However, it appeared that a “Beng Ramos” (Divina Gracia V. Ramos) is employed in the Office of the Secretary, particularly under the Blue Ribbon Oversight Office Management.
VERA Files has debunked other disinformation related to the flood control issue.
At least 10 posts by FB users and pages collectively garnered 1,140 reactions, 163 comments and 62 shares.
author: VERA Files
claim: Sen. Jinggoy Estrada called former Department of Public Works and Highways assistant district engineer Brice Hernandez “a boy in a man’s world.”

title: Video shows protest in Nepal, NOT in PH
content:
A clip posted on Facebook claims to show footage of a violent protest that allegedly took place in the Philippines. This is false.
The video was taken in Kathmandu, Nepal during a youth-led demonstration.
Published on Sept. 13, the 18-second video captured from an elevated vantage point shows a fire truck firing a water cannon to disperse protesters. It contained a superimposed text that read:
“oh my goodnesses, ganito kagulo ang rally ng piLipinAs (Oh my goodness. This is how chaotic the rally is in the Philippines)”
The erroneous post also carried a caption that stated:
“ganito ung rally naganap sa pilipinas (This is the rally that happened in the Philippines).”
A reverse image search using keyframes of the FB reel reveals that the clip is from a longer video first posted by a user on TikTok on Sept. 8, 2025. The caption of the original video included the hashtags #GenZ and #Nepal. The misleading clip was taken starting at the 00:14 mark.
Nepal-based digital media outlet Setopati and Indian news outfit NDTV also posted footage of the same protest from a different angle. According to Setopati, tear gas and water cannons were used by authorities after protesters attempted to enter the premises of the Parliamentary House.
On Sept. 8, Nepali protesters, most of them from Generation Z, flooded the streets of Kathmandu to oppose a ban on major social media platforms and to demand action on widespread corruption and poor economic opportunities. The demonstration escalated as protesters set fire to government establishments, including the Parliament building and the Supreme Court.
The incorrect video surfaced after protests also erupted across Metro Manila on Sept. 12 denouncing the anomalies in flood control projects that allegedly involved officials from the Department of Public Works and Highways and certain politicians.
Posted by Facebook user Rachelle Lumactod (created May 20, 2023), the false reel has so far earned 137,000 views, 4,500 reactions, 353 shares, and 192 comments as of writing.
author: VERA Files
claim: A clip shows a violent protest that occured in the Philippines.

title: Video of protest action in Kenya PASSED OFF as ‘EDSA’ rally in PH
content:
Amid several anti-corruption rallies this September, yet another video supposedly showing a protest action in the Philippines is circulating online. This is false. The video was taken in Kenya in 2024.
On Sept. 14, a Facebook user posted the 11-second clip showing a massive crowd of protesters.
The post carried the text:
“Halaka kilos prostesta (sic) siguro ito, EDSA 3. Kaya sana lahat safe (Oh no. This is probably a protest action, EDSA 3. So I hope everyone is safe).”
The caption used the hashtag “#BangonPilipinas”
A copy reposted by another FB user bore the additional text:
“Oh my goodness. Sobrang dami ng tao, para lang mga langgam. Susugod na yan (There are so many people, they look like ants. They’re about to push forward).”
This is also false. The video shows the same 2024 protest in Kenya.
TikTok user and film maker @Dannigiza uploaded the original clip on June 20, 2024. The post’s caption read:
“Nakuru well represented #rejectfainancebill2024 #Nakuru #kenya.”
On that day, protests erupted in various areas of Kenya to push back against their government’s plans of implementing tax hikes to address budget deficiencies.
VERA Files Fact Check geolocated the clip using visual landmarks and confirmed that the protest took place in Kenyatta Ln, Nakuru, Kenya.
The same video also circulated last year with a different context.
The false posts appeared this September two days after the Armed Forces of the Philippines was placed on red alert amid several anti-corruption rallies in the country.
Groups are set to gather on Sept. 21 to protest against corruption, specifically in the government’s anomalous flood control projects. The event coincides with the 53rd anniversary of Martial Law declaration.
VERA Files has debunked other videos falsely attributed to the anti-corruption protests this month.
The posts by at least two FB users collectively garnered 24,600 reactions, 1,279 comments, 4,218 shares and 914,000 views.
author: VERA Files
claim: Video shows an “EDSA” protest in the Philippines.

title: 2024 news report edited to FALSELY claim Duterte has passed away
content:
A Facebook reel showing a news clip from Net 25 claims that former president Rodrigo Duterte, who has been detained at The Hague, Netherlands, has passed away. This is not true.
The video used an edited news report aired in 2024 to deceive viewers into thinking that it was about Duterte’s death.
Published on Sept. 13, the 7-second video shows Net 25 anchor Alex Santos supposedly reporting the passing of the former president, saying:
“Patay na si dating pangulong Rodrigo Duterte, ayon po yan kay Senador Christopher Bong Go. (Former president Rodrigo Duterte has died, according to Sen. Christopher Bong Go).”
The video included a superimposed text that read: “Duterte patay na (Duterte is dead).” In the caption, the user wrote:
“HALA REST IN PEACE, Pumanaw nadaw si former President Duterte ngayon lang, Kung totoo man eto Rest in Peace, If hindi man, Thank you dahil you still need to face your trial in court before facing the trial in heaven
(SO REST IN PEACE, Former president Duterte has reportedly died just now. If this is true, Rest in Peace. If not, thank you because you still need to face your trial in court before facing trial in heaven).“
A combination of reverse image and keyword searches led to a news report posted by Net 25 on TikTok and YouTube on June 6, 2024, when rumors about Duterte’s passing were circulating.
The introduction of the original report was cut. In the original report, Santos said: “Fake news ang kumakalat sa social media na patay na si dating pangulong Rodrigo Duterte, ayon po ‘yan kay Senador Christopher Bong Go.”
Also omitted was the latter part of the report, which showed footage of Go with Duterte in his house in Davao City, where the former president appeared alive and in good health.
In a Facebook post, the official page of Net 25 debunked the claim and called it fake.
“Mariin po naming pinasisinungalingan ang videong ito. Ito ay palsipikado, mapanlinlang at sinadyang baguhin upang magpakalat ng maling impormasyon at kalituhan sa ating mga kababayan,” the network wrote.
(We vehemently deny this video. This is falsified, misleading and deliberately altered to spread wrong information and confusion among the people.)
It also urged the public to be cautious and to avoid sharing unverified information.
On Sept. 8, the International Criminal Court postponed the Sept. 23 confirmation of charges hearing for Duterte after his lawyers requested an indefinite adjournment, claiming he was “not fit to stand trial” due to cognitive impairment and difficulty recalling events.
However, Veronica “Kitty” Duterte, the youngest daughter of the former president, said on Sept. 12 that her father is “alive and well.”
Posted by Facebook page Byaheng Filipinos (Nov. 4, 2021), the false reel has so far garnered 1.4 million views, 2,300 reactions, 2,100 comments, and 619 shares. Another copy was shared by Facebook user Joyce Anne Atlata (created Dec. 16, 2013), which earned 5.2 million views, 13,400 reactions, 8,000 comments, and 1,300 shares as of writing.
author: VERA Files
claim: A news report from Net25 says former president Rodrigo Duterte has passed away.

title: Video shows 2025 Peñafrancia Traslacion, NOT rally vs Marcos, Romualdez, Co
content:
A Facebook user posted a video showing an influx of people supposedly calling for the resignation of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., House Speaker Martin Romualdez and Ako Bicol Party-list Rep. Elizaldy Co. This is false.
The video showed devotees, pilgrims and visitors flocking to Naga City, Camarines Sur for the 2025 Traslacion procession of the images of Our Lady of Peñafrancia, the patroness of the Bicol Region, and El Divino Rostro or the Face of Jesus, on Sept. 12.
The video with false context, uploaded on Sept. 14, bore an overlaid text that read:
“Sigaw ng Taong Bayan Marcos Resign
MARTIN ROMUALDEZ ZALDY CO Resign
Gulong gulo na Ang Pilipinas Nasaan Ang president walang Action
(Cry of the people: Marcos resign
Martin Romualdez, Zaldy Co resign
The Philippines is in chaos. Where is the president? No action)”
Its caption stated:
“Share kulang. Sa nangyari ngayun nasaan na kaya SI PBBM. (Just sharing. With what happened today, where could PBBM be?)”
A reverse image search reveals that the video was originally published by radio station DZRH News on Sept. 12, showing devotees passing by the Peñafrancia Avenue during the Traslacion procession in Naga City.
The inaccurate post surfaced following widespread protests across Metro Manila on Sept. 12 and 13 where students, activists and other rallyists denounced corruption in government projects, particularly those involving flood control structures.
In a Sept. 15 press conference, Marcos said he does not blame the public for staging demonstrations against the massive corruption in government, adding, “If I [weren’t] president, I might be out on the streets with them.”
In the same briefing, the president assured the public that Romualdez, his first cousin, and other allies in the House of Representatives and the Senate “will not be spared” from the investigation by the newly formed Independent Commission for Infrastructure.
Under Executive Order No. 94, the ICI is mandated to investigate anomalies, irregularities and misuse of funds in government flood control and other infrastructure projects within the last decade.
During the Sept. 8 Senate hearing, Navotas Rep. Toby Tiangco claimed that Co, former chair of the House Committee on Appropriations, was the proponent of some P13.8 billion worth of insertions for flood control and other infrastructure projects in the 2025 budget.
In the same congressional probe, contractor-couple Pacifico “Curlee” Discaya II and Cezarah “Sarah” Discaya, in their sworn statement, alleged that Romualdez and Co were mentioned to them by one politician and some officials of the Department of Public Works and Highways in relation to obtaining kickbacks from infrastructure projects.
However, Curlee Discaya clarified during the Sept. 9 House hearing that he did not have any personal transactions with the two lawmakers. Romualdez and Co have both denied the accusations of the Discaya couple.
Published by FB user Bo Yaks (created on April 11, 2023), the video has been viewed over 1.7 million times as of writing. It has so far garnered 42,400 reactions, 6,300 comments and 8,500 shares.
author: VERA Files
claim: A video shows an influx of people calling for the resignation of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., House Speaker Martin Romualdez and AKO Bicol Party-list Rep. Elizaldy Co.

title: Video shows INC peace rally, NOT ‘people power’ protest to oust Marcos
content:
A video showing a huge gathering, supposedly a protest rally calling for the ouster of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., is circulating online. This is false. The video was taken from a religious event in Manila last January.
On Sept. 13, a TikTok user posted a 48-second drone footage of the supposed “people power” rally attended by a massive crowd wearing shirts. An audio clip of a man reporting on the supposed gatherings in Davao and the simultaneous protests in Mendiola was overlaid.
The video bore the caption:
“GOD SAVE THE PHILIPPINES #peoplepower3🇵🇭 #2025 #corrupt #trendviralvideo #fyppppppppppppppppppppppp.”
In the audio, a man can be heard saying:
“Habang kayo po ay nagtitipon tipon diyan sa Davao, may mga pwersa na po na papunta ng Malacanang din maya maya lang. Mendiola na para yanigin po ang Malacanang ni Marcos Jr. para lumayas sa poder. Ito may mga panawagan na. Nananawagan na po ng Black Day Protest ngayong Linggo ng gabi. Ipapanganak ang pangatlong lunto ng kasaysayan sa pilipinas upang bawiiin ang gobyerno sa kamay ng mga abusado, kriminal at utak sindikato na uri ng gobyerno. So this time around it’s not only in Davao, Admar ah. Nananawagan ang maraming grupo ngayon sa Metro Manila na Mendiola na. Hindi na EDSA. Sa Mendiola na at doon na mag-vigil ang taong bayan in solidarity with what is happening right now in Davao na parang laboratoryo na ng Martial Law. At kung paano ginahasa ang karapatan at kalayaan ng mamamayan
(While you are gathering there in Davao, there are forces that are already on their way to Malacanang soon. It is in Mendiola now to shake down Marcos Jr.’s Malacanang and get him out of power. There are calls already. They are already calling for a Black Day Protest this Sunday night. The third wave of history in the Philippines will be born to take back the government from the hands of the abusive, criminal and syndicate-minded type of government. So this time around it’s not only in Davao, Admar. Many groups in Metro Manila are now calling that it should be in Mendiola now. Not EDSA. It should be in Mendiola and the people should hold a vigil there in solidarity with what is happening right now in Davao, which is like a laboratory of Martial Law. And how the rights and freedoms of the people have been violated)….”
Other Facebook users posted copies of the video. A reader sent one of the posts to the VERA Files Misinformation Tipline for verification.
These posts are false. The video shows the Iglesia ni Cristo’s National Peace Rally at the Quirino Grandstand in Manila last January, not a “people power” movement for Marcos’ ouster.
On Jan. 13, TikTok user @tagabayo uploaded the original video bearing the hashtag #nationalrallyforpeace and location tag “Quirino Grandstand, Manila.”
The TikTok user also warned others about posts that falsely attributed the clip of the peace rally to the supposed political movement.
According to the INC, the Jan. 13 peace rally, which reportedly drew around 1.5 million members, was moral and not political. While INC spokesperson Edwil Zabala said the aim of the rally was for the country’s peace, they did express support for Marcos’ previous stance against the impeachment of Vice President Sara Duterte.
The false video circulated on the same day that anti-corruption groups held a protest march along EDSA Shrine to the People Power Monument in Quezon City. This was in relation to the ongoing investigations into the government’s anomalous flood control projects.
The Duterte camp’s Hakbang ng Maisug movement also protested at the People Power Monument the same day. Days prior, social media pages showing support for the Dutertes posted a call for “people power 3” on EDSA last Sept. 13.
VERA Files has debunked other religious activities passed off as political rallies.
The post by TikTok user @boss_ra808 garnered 109,000 reactions, 9,505 comments, 24,600 shares and 2,600,000 views. Five copies reposted by FB users garnered additional 45,554 reactions, 4,300 comments, 12,180 shares and 1,802,300 views.
author: VERA Files
claim: Video shows massive rally for the ouster of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.

title: DPWH job hiring post FAKE, used to promote e-commerce sites
content:
A Facebook page posted an erroneous graphics card claiming that the Department of Public Works and Highways is hiring for various positions, with no experience required for some items. This is fake.
Published on Sept. 11, the photo bore the logo of DPWH with the following text:
“DPWH NOW HIRING. NO EXPERIENCE NEEDED FOR SOME POSITIONS”
It also claimed that the application period is open until Sept. 30, 2025, with two links provided where those interested can apply. The caption of the fake post partly read:
“JOB OPPORTUNITY ALERT!! The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) is now hiring for multiple Engineering, Finance, Janitorial, Job order and Administrative positions.”
The links provided in the clickbait post redirect to a sports product listed on two different e-commerce platforms.
An earlier post shared in the Facebook group “Homebased Job Philippines” on Aug. 27 said that the department is recruiting a project manager, administrative assistant, and engineer with relevant college degrees.
A quick review of the DPWH’s official social media pages shows no such hiring post.
At the end of the caption of the Sept. 11 post is the word “satire”. Although the publisher does not generally post satirical content, it has previously shared fake announcements of class suspensions.
The post surfaced after the DPWH became embroiled in controversy over anomalous flood control projects. Investigations conducted by the Senate blue ribbon committee revealed that some officials of the agency, along with some politicians, had been allegedly receiving kickbacks from these projects.
Posted by Facebook page May Pasok Ba? (created June 21, 2025), the post has garnered 1,700 shares, 129 reactions, and 33 comments as of writing. Another copy published by a user in an FB group has earned 817 shares, 681 reactions, and 48 comments.
author: VERA Files
claim: The Department of Public Works and Highways posted an advertisement for available jobs that require no work experience

title: NO $45-M Wilson endorsement offer to Alex Eala
content:
Similar posts on Facebook, Instagram and TikTok are circulating claiming that international sports brand Wilson offered a $45-million endorsement deal to rising Filipina tennis player Alexandra Eala which she has accepted. This is fake.
One of the earliest posts, published on Sept. 3 by an FB fan page, carried the following caption:
“Alexandra Eala has shocked the world and the Philippine media by accepting Wilson’s $45 million endorsement offer, a brand that dominates the tennis racket market: ‘Thank you for trusting me, but I will take that money and use it to do something that truly helps others, because there are many people who need it more than I do, and I want the Philippines to continue growing stronger.’ At the same time, she announced that she would use the money for something special..”
The post included a link in the comments section, which opens to another website where an entire article, dated Sept. 4, appears.
The post is a hoax. There is no official statement from Wilson Sporting Goods or Eala about such a deal. There are no news reports about it either; only similar posts on social media.
The quote attributed to the 20-year-old tennis player in the caption is false. It was taken from another erroneous endorsement deal with a golf player, that also linked an article to the same website that featured the supposed Wilson-Eala deal.
Presently, Eala’s sponsors include sports brands Nike and Babolat, as well as Filipino companies of the Ayala Group – Bank of the Philippine Islands and Globe Telecoms – all of which she follows on social media. There are no reports that she endorses any brand.
Additionally, the photo at the bottom right is the LinkedIn profile of Joe Dudy, former president and CEO of Wilson, who resigned on Aug. 31, 2025, three days before the post was surfaced.
Created on March 16, 2025, the FB fan page Tennis Plus posted the report which garnered more than 103,000 likes, 6,700 comments and 3,100 shares as of writing.
Editor’s note: This fact check was produced with the help of a journalism student of the University of Santo Tomas as part of their internship at VERA Files.
author: VERA Files
claim: Sports brand Wilson offered a $45-million endorsement deal to Alexandra Eala, who reportedly accepted this.

title: NO P10,000 monthly 4Ps allowance from VP Sara
content:
A Facebook post is claiming that Vice President Sara Duterte is giving an additional P10,000 to all beneficiaries of the Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps). This is fake.
Published on Sept. 9 by an FB page, the post that continues to circulate this week bears a photo of the vice president, with text that says:
“Dagdag na P10,000 sa lahat ng beneficiaries ng 4Ps rekta sa ATM at OTC mula sa ating VP Presidente.”
The graphic also carries the logo of the Land Bank of the Philippines.
There is no announcement from the Department of Social Welfare and Development or the Office of the Vice President about an additional cash grant of P10,000 for 4Ps beneficiaries.
Under Republic Act 11310, which institutionalized the 4Ps, grantees are entitled to a monthly conditional cash transfer.
Benefits include a health and nutrition grant (P750), education grant for daycare and elementary students (P300); junior high school students (P500); and senior high school students (P700).
During the COVID-19 pandemic, DSWD recommended an increase in financial assistance for beneficiaries, but this has yet to materialize.
The post instructs netizens to click the links attached in the caption to check if their names, regions, and unconditional cash transfer payout schedules are included in the list of beneficiaries from the vice president.
The accompanying links take netizens to a site filled with ads. Numerous netizens were deceived by the post, as many expressed hope to be included and asked for details in the comment section.
The names of 4Ps beneficiaries are not shared online, but posted in public spaces within their localities.
DSWD had corrected similar claims in 2023 that all 4Ps beneficiaries of social protection programs are only identified through Listahan, a “database of poor households” to identify potential recipients of social services.
Facebook page DSWD News Update All Region (created on Aug. 2, 2025), published the claim, collectively garnering over 101 reactions, 51 comments, and 226 shares.
Editor’s note: This fact check was produced with the help of a journalism student of the University of Santo Tomas as part of their internship at VERA Files.
author: VERA Files
claim: Vice President Sara Duterte is giving an additional P10,000 to all Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps) beneficiaries.

title: Circulating ‘graphic’ of Discaya exposing CDO City Mayor FAKE
content:
A news graphic claimed that controversial contractor Sarah Discaya named Cagayan de Oro City Mayor Rolando “Klarex” Uy as among those who received money from them. This is fabricated.
On Sept. 8, a Facebook page uploaded a social media card with the logo “News5 Northern Mindanao” as well as photos of Discaya and Uy. The headline read:
“Discaya maipamatuod nga gadawat si Klarey Uy og 25% commission sa mga proyekto gikan 2016 taman 2022 (Discaya confirmed that Klarey Uy received a 25% commission from projects from 2016 to 2022).”
Discaya was quoted as supposedly saying:
“Tumanggap po si Klarex Uy mula sa amin. 25% ang hiningi nya (sic) (Klarex Uy received (money) from us. He asked for 25%).”
This is fake. News5 did not release such a graphic and Discaya did not make that statement in previous congressional hearings.
News5 did not publish that graphic on their official social media pages and website.
The news organization does not use a “News5 Northern Mindanao” logo. The text “Northern Mindanao” was simply added to News5’s official logo.
Discaya’s photo was taken during the Sept. 1, 2025 Senate inquiry on alleged corruption in the government’s flood control projects. She never mentioned Uy in the hearing.
To further deceive netizens, the page uploaded an edited photo of the sworn statement of Sarah and her husband Curlee Discaya in the comment section. The couple’s joint statement, released on Sept. 8 during another Senate Blue Ribbon Committee session, did not contain Uy’s name.
The sworn statement only listed congressmen and Department of Public Works and Highways officials as those allegedly involved in the corrupt activities.
The page altered the photo by replacing the name of “Cong. Jojo Ang of Uswag Ilonggo Partylist” to “Mayor Rolando ‘Klarex’ Uy of Cagayan de Oro City.”
The Cagayan De Oro City Information Office also released an advisory calling the graphic “fake news.”
There are several flood control projects in Cagayan de Oro City listed on President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.’s Sumbong sa Pangulo website. However, none of these were handled by firms owned by Sarah Discaya.
The fake graphic circulated the same day the Discayas attended another Senate Blue Ribbon Committee’s inquiry on the flood control projects.
VERA Files has debunked other disinformation related to the controversial contractor.
The post by the FB page CDO Now (created on May 19, 2025) garnered 4,300 reactions, 909 comments and 1,400 shares. A copy posted on the FB group Cagayan de Oro City (created on Feb. 24, 2015) also received 83 reactions, 28 comments and 14 shares.
author: VERA Files
claim: Sarah Discaya named Cagayan de Oro City Mayor Rolando “Klarex” Uy as among those who received money from them.

title: Reel on divorce bill approval in PH NEEDS CONTEXT
content:
A Facebook reel claims that the long-pending divorce bill has finally been approved in the Philippines. This needs context.
While the House of Representatives did pass an absolute divorce bill in 2024, the Senate had not done so with its counterpart bill by the time the 19th Congress ended last June.
No divorce measure has been passed in the current 20th Congress.
Uploaded on Sept. 1, the reel bore a superimposed text that read:
“Congratulations sa lahat ng nangangarap nito! Divorce bill 9349 finally approved. Mag saya na ang lahat ng kabet.”
(Congratulations to all who are dreaming of this! Divorce bill 9349 finally approved. Be happy all illicit lovers).
Divorce remains illegal in the country as both chambers of Congress — the House and the Senate — must ratify a bill and submit it to the Office of the President to become law.
The House approved House Bill No. 9349 or the Absolute Divorce Act on third reading on May 22, 2024. However, the Senate failed to act on any of the five divorce bills that stalled at the committee level for the entire 19th Congress from 2022 to 2025.
Pending bills are terminated upon the term conclusion of the Congress where it was filed. This means the divorce bill would have to be refiled in the 20th Congress for a chance to become a law.
There are six House bills and two Senate bills legalizing divorce filed in the current Congress, all of which are pending at the committee level.
Some netizens who commented on the post praised lawmakers for allowing divorce in the country and asked where they could file for one.
Posted by FB page BuzzedTv (created May 21, 2025), the reel has so far received 3.2 million interactions.
author: VERA Files
claim: The divorce bill has finally been approved in the Philippines

title: Lacson is new Senate blue ribbon chair, NOT Hontiveros
content:
A YouTube video claims that Sen. Risa Hontiveros has replaced Sen. Rodante Marcoleta as chairperson of the Senate blue ribbon committee, following a leadership shakeup in the Upper Chamber early this week. This is not true.
It was Sen. Panfilo Lacson who was elected chairperson of that body formally known as the Committee on Accountability of Public Officers and Investigations last Sept. 9.
Uploaded on Sept. 8, the video’s thumbnail partly read:
“CONFIRMED! Hontiveros replaces Marcoleta as Chair of the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee.”
It also carried the headline: “HONTIVEROS PAPALIT KAY MARCOLETA? WALA NA PAG-ASA ANG PILIPINAS (Hontiveros replacing Marcoleta? There is no hope for the Philippines).”
Lacson, recently elected Senate President Pro tempore, replaced Marcoleta as chair of the blue ribbon committee. The former police chief will lead the Senate investigation on anomalous flood control projects across the country.
Hontiveros, who is now deputy majority leader, is new chairperson of the Committee on Health taking over Sen. Bong Go. She was offered to lead the blue ribbon committee but declined as she preferred Lacson to take the post, according to Senate President Vicente Sotto III.
Uploaded by YouTube channel Robin Sweet Showbiz, the erroneous video received 16,253 interactions.
author: VERA Files
claim: Sen. Risa Hontiveros is the new chairperson of the Senate blue ribbon committee

title: U.S. government did NOT junk cases vs. Quiboloy
content:
An old claim has resurfaced online alleging that the United States has dropped the cases against Kingdom of Jesus Christ founder Apollo Quiboloy, who was indicted in 2021 by a federal jury on charges including sexual abuse and trafficking. This is false.
Posted on Aug. 23, a 20-second Facebook reel featured a post containing a graphics card with text that read:
“Korte sa Amerika, ibinasura ang mga kasong human trafficking, forced labor, at money laundering vs KOJC Leader
(U.S. court, dismissed cases of human trafficking, forced labor, and money laundering vs KOJ leader)“
The erroneous post was captioned:
“Korte sa America ibinasura ang mga kaso ni Pastor Quiboloy.
(U.S. court dismissed cases against Pastor Quiboloy)”
Some netizens expressed thanks and congratulated the pastor in the comments section. The post has circulated on FB, TikTok and YouTube.
There are no credible reports confirming that the U.S. government has dropped the charges against Quiboloy.
As of Sept. 2025, the televangelist remains on the Most Wanted list of the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation, according to the record on its official website.
Quiboloy is wanted for his “alleged participation in a labor trafficking scheme that brought church members to the United States, via fraudulently obtained visas, and forced the members to solicit donations for a bogus charity, donations that actually were used to finance church operations and the lavish lifestyles of its leaders,” based on the FBI.
Only the charges against his co-accused and KOJC administrator Marissa Dueñas, who entered a plea bargain agreement in exchange for a lighter sentence last October 2024, were dismissed.
On Nov. 10, 2021, the United States District Court for the Central District of California issued a federal warrant against Quiboloy after he was indicted for conspiracy to engage in sex trafficking by force, fraud, and coercion and bulk cash smuggling, among other charges.
He is also facing separate charges of qualified human trafficking before a Pasig City court and sexual abuse before a Quezon City court.
The false post surfaced after the Philippine Ambassador to the U.S. Jose Manuel Romualdez confirmed on Aug. 20 that the U.S. has sent a formal request to the Philippines to extradite Quiboloy. However, the DOJ said it has not yet received such a request.
VERA Files has previously debunked a false post claiming the dismissal of his case (READ: Apollo Quiboloy is NOT the KOJC leader with dismissed charges).
Posted by Facebook page Tapang at Malasakit Global (created Oct. 22, 2017), the reel has so far garnered 5,400 views, 252 reactions, 85 shares and 22 comments.
author: VERA Files
claim: The United States has dismissed the charges against televangelist and Kingdom of Jesus Christ founder Apollo Quiboloy.

title: Remulla NOT named new Ombudsman
content:
A YouTube video claims that Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin “Boying” Remulla is the new Ombudsman. Not true.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has yet to select a new ombudsman as the Judicial and Bar Council has not even released its shortlist of nominees for the position.
Uploaded on Sept. 1, the video’s headline read:
“REMULLA NA ANG NEW OMBUDSMAN! MAY NAGLEAKED! (Remulla is the new Ombudsman! Someone leaked it!)”
Dante Vargas is currently Acting Ombudsman after Samuel Martires’ seven-year term ended on July 27.
The JBC concluded its public interviews of applicants for the position only on Sept. 2, a day after the video surfaced. After deliberations, the JBC will release a shortlist of nominees for Ombudsman from which the president will choose who to appoint.
The video emerged a day after Sen. Imee Marcos claimed that Vargas is being bribed to dismiss a case she filed against Remulla in the Office of the Ombudsman for the arrest and turnover of former president Rodrigo Duterte to the International Criminal Court in The Hague..
Supreme Court Spokesperson Camille Ting said Remulla must be cleared of this pending case before the end of deliberations “in the next few weeks” so he can qualify as a shortlisted nominee.
Remulla said he will file a motion to resolve the case “as soon as possible.”
Uploaded by YouTube channel Robin Sweet Showbiz, the erroneous video received 13,346 interactions.
VERA Files Fact Check previously debunked a claim that Remulla has been disqualified from the Ombudsman race.
author: VERA Files
claim: Justice Secretary Jesus Crispin “Boying” Remulla is the new Ombudsman

title: Villanueva did NOT resign as senator
content:
A Facebook post claims that Senate Majority Leader Joel Villanueva has resigned after his photos and videos in the company of a Public Works engineer who had been tagged in anomalous flood control projects have gone viral.
This is not true. There has been no such announcement from Villanueva, who remains a member of the 20th Congress.
Published on Aug. 28, the post read:
“KAKAPASOK LANG! VILLANUEVA NAIYAK TULUYAN NG NAGPAALAM, NAG-RESIGN NA SA PWESTO,BUKING NA NI PBBM.”
(Just in! Villanueva cried, ultimately bid goodbye, resigned from his position, caught by PBBM).
Villanueva has not resigned as a senator. He was still majority leader at the Senate’s plenary session on Sept. 3.
The senator from Bulacan came under fire after photos and videos of him with Henry Alcantara, formerly a district engineer of the Department of Public Works and Highways in the province, surfaced and went viral on social media.
Alcantara admitted in a Sept. 2 public hearing at the House of Representatives that he had signed contracts of P224.9 million worth of “ghost” and substandard flood control projects in Baliwag City and Calumpit town.
In a Sept. 3 interview, Villanueva denied having transactions with Alcantara, saying that his photos with Alcantara that have gone viral were taken during a public event and that other government officials also have photos with the district engineer when they visit DPWH projects in Bulacan . He added:
“Yung sinasabi niyong video, picture, lahat ng opisyal may video [at] picture sa kanya. Official po ‘yun, eh. So ‘pag pumunta ka sa DPWH sa Bulacan — si presidente, mga secretary, mga senador — lahat meron. Pero bakit ako lang ‘yung sini-single out?”
(The video, picture you’re saying, all officials have that with him. He’s an official. So if you go to DPWH in Bulacan — whether the president, secretaries or senators — everybody has that. Why are they singling me out?)
Source: Bilyonaryo News Channel Facebook page, WATCH: Sen. Joel Villanueva eyes legal action …, Sept. 3, 2025 (4:40 to 5:00)
Posted by FB pages Filipina Glitz, which is being managed from Vietnam and the United States, as well as NL Star Buzz and Celebrity Updates, the erroneous post has so far received 954 interactions.
author: VERA Files
claim: Joel Villanueva has resigned from the Philippine Senate

title: Stealing of public funds NOT a crime against humanity
content:
Musician-turned-lawyer Jimmy Bondoc has claimed that stealing public funds intended for flood control is a crime against humanity and falls under the jurisdiction of the International Criminal Court in The Hague. This is false.
In a Facebook post dated Aug. 30, Bondoc said:
“Opinion: ang pagnakaw ng flood control funds ay crime against humanity. Saklaw yan ng ICC.”
(Opinion: Stealing of flood control funds is a crime against humanity. That is under the jurisdiction of the ICC.)
Source: Jimmy Bondoc Facebook page, Opinion: ang pagnakaw ng flood control funds ay crime against humanity…, Aug. 30, 2025
Three days later, he elaborated on his “opinion,” saying in a mix of English and Filipino:
“Is the stealing of flood control funds systematic? Yes, we’ve seen the massive collusion. Does it cause great suffering? Does it torture our people? And finally, is it intentional? You stole the funds even though you know that doing so will flood the country. I would say that that is definitely intentional.”
Source: Jimmy Bondoc Facebook page, Opinion: Crimes Againt Humanity examples…, Sept. 2, 2025
Bondoc was assistant vice president for entertainment at the Philippine Amusement and Gaming Corporation during the Duterte administration.
Stealing of public funds and corruption do not qualify as crimes against humanity as defined by the Rome Statute which established the ICC.
Section 7 of the treaty defines crimes against humanity as specific acts committed as “part of a widespread or systematic attack directed against any civilian population, with knowledge of the attack.”
These acts include murder, extermination, enslavement, deportation, torture, unlawful imprisonment, various forms of sexual violence, persecution, enforced disappearance, apartheid, and other inhumane acts of similar character which intentionally cause suffering or injury.
The Rome Statute further clarifies that a widespread or systematic attack directed against any civilian population means that it is not a random series of attacks and should be committed in accordance with a State or organizational policy.
Since the Philippines withdrew its membership from the ICC on March 17, 2019, the international tribunal has jurisdiction only over crimes committed in the country when it was still a member. The same rule was applied in the case of former president Rodrigo Duterte, who has been detained since March 12 in The Hague for alleged crimes against humanity in relation to the extrajudicial killings during his administration’s brutal war against illegal drugs.
In a Viber message to VERA Files, international law expert Romel Bagares described Bondoc’s claim as “a political statement for emphasis, rather than one that is legally aligned with the Rome Statute.”
He explained that anomalies on the flood control projects may not fall under ICC’s definition of crimes against humanity and emphasized that the Philippines already has existing laws addressing plunder and graft and corruption.
Bondoc, who ran for senator but lost in the May 12 midterm elections under the PDP-Laban slate endorsed by Duterte, previously claimed that the ICC has no jurisdiction in the Philippines and opposed the prospect of the country rejoining the ICC.
Jimmy Bondoc Facebook page, Opinion: ang pagnakaw ng flood control funds ay crime against humanity…, Aug. 30, 2025
Jimmy Bondoc Facebook page, Opinion: Crimes Againt Humanity examples…, Sept. 2, 2025
International Criminal Court, Rome Statute of the International Court, accessed Sept. 2, 2025
Atty. Romel Bagares, Personal Communication, Sept. 2, 2025
GMA News, Jimmy Bondoc opposes rejoining ICC, says prosecuting Duterte on EJKs up to local courts, Feb. 2, 2025
Commentary
News
FACT CHECK
author: VERA FILES
claim: Stealing public funds intended for flood control is a crime against humanity and falls under the jurisdiction of the International Criminal Court.

title: Old video MISREPRESENTS as Aug. 30 flooding in Quezon City
content:
In the wake of a severe thunderstorm that hit the northern portions of Metro Manila on Aug. 30, a video circulating on Facebook claims to show footage of a raging flood supposedly on Quezon Avenue in Quezon City. This is false.
This is an old clip taken in Talisay, Batangas during the onslaught of Typhoon Kristine in 2024.
Published on Aug. 31, the 23-second video shows vehicles being swept away by strong currents of floodwaters. It has an overlaid text that read:
“Bumuhos ang malakas na baha dito sa Quezon avenue city”
The post has a caption saying:
“Mga sasakyan tinangay ng malakas na Baha. #flood #news #latest“
A reverse image search using Google led to a video posted by local radio station Brigada Batangas on Oct. 24, 2024. The original video of the raging flood has a caption saying that it was taken in Barangay Sampaloc, Talisay, Batangas during Typhoon Kristine.
Radyo Pilipinas Lucena shared the same video on the same day. It wrote:
“PANUORIN: Natangay ng baha ang ilang mga sasakyan habang rumaragasa ang tubig na may kasamang putik, mga troso at bato sa kalsada sa Barangay Sampaloc, Talisay, Batangas kasabay ng malakas na ulan dulot ng Bagyong Kristine kahapon.”
Both radio channels credited the video to a Facebook user who first uploaded the original clip in Oct. 2024.
The false video surfaced following the heavy torrential rain that caused severe flooding in many parts of Quezon City on Aug. 30.
Posted by Facebook page Kugihang BATA (created July 27, 2025), the false video has so far garnered 5.6 million views, 52,600 reactions, 15,000 shares, and 12,900 comments.
author: VERA Files
claim: A video shows footage of recent flooding in Quezon City.

title: NO senator removed by Marcos amid flood control issue
content:
A Facebook post and a website article are spreading the erroneous claim that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has decided to “remove” Sens. Francis “Chiz” Escudero, Robin Padilla and Joel Villanueva from office. This is false.
On Aug. 22, an FB page published the post and included a link to the spurious website with the same false claim. The caption read:
“JUST IN! Chiz Escudero, Padilla, and Villanueva break down in tears as they bid farewell — after President Marcos Jr. delivers the shocking decision to remove them from power! What really happened behind closed doors?”
The unnamed author of the linked website article further added that the “bombshell decision” came out of nowhere, shocking staff members in Congress as they allegedly witnessed the three senators “visibly shaken” following the announcement of their supposed removal.
This is not true. The three senators remain in their elected positions and are not facing any move to remove them.
The president has no power to suspend or expel an elected lawmaker. According to Article VI, Section 16 of the 1987 Constitution, only senators can expel a fellow member in the Senate following a two-thirds vote.
This post surfaced amid the flood control projects controversy. Sen. Panfilo “Ping” Lacson said in an Aug. 23 interview that senators may be involved in the anomalous flood control projects through their power to propose insertions in the national budget.
In an exclusive Politiko report, Escudero was said to have inserted P142.7 billion in the 2025 General Appropriations Act. Villanueva’s bailiwick, Bulacan, was the biggest recipient of insertions worth over P12 billion in infrastructure allotments.
FB page Magsasaka, Mabuhay Ka (created on July 18, 2017) published the post with the made-up claim, garnering over 970 reactions, 730 comments and 50 shares. The article was posted on a website called newspro.celebtoday24h.com, which is managed from the United States with content focusing on issues in the Philippines.
author: VERA Files
claim: President Ferdinand Marcos removed Sens. Chiz Escudero, Robin Padilla and Joel Villanueva from their positions.

title: NO Senate contempt order against Vice Ganda
content:
A YouTube video suggests that the Senate cited Vice Ganda in contempt after she joked about Sen. Rodante Marcoleta at her concert earlier this month. This is false.
No such order came from the Senate. Vice Ganda has not been invited to attend nor was her name explicitly mentioned at a Senate committee hearing on online gambling platforms last Aug. 14.
Uploaded on Aug. 17, the video’s headline read:
“KAKAPASOK LANG! C0NTEMPT sa HEARING ng SENADO? MARCOLETA T1NAP0S si VICE GANDA BILIS ng KAR MA?”
(Just in! Contempt in the Senate hearing? Marcoleta ended Vice Ganda, karma is so fast?)
There was no motion from any senator to cite Vice Ganda in contempt, nor was she invited to a committee hearing in the first place.
Under Senate rules, a committee chairman with the concurrence of one senator may cite a resource person in contempt for testifying falsely or refusing to follow the committee’s orders during a legislative inquiry. An individual cited in contempt will be detained in the Senate under the custody of the sergeant-at-arms.
During the Aug. 14 committee hearing, Marcoleta, without naming names, expressed his ire toward a certain comedian for making fun of his physical appearance in a recent concert sponsored by an online gaming platform. He said:
“Pati po ako ay kanyang idinawit. Bigla ba naman niyang sinabi, ‘Tignan niyo yung mukha ni Marcoleta kung matatawa kayo. Napaka walang hiya noong tao na ‘yon. Ayaw ko na lang siyang patusin.“
(She dragged me into it as well. She suddenly said, “Look at Marcoleta’s face and let’s see if you will laugh.” That person is so shameless. I don’t even want to bother dealing with her anymore.)
A clip of Vice Ganda joking about Marcoleta’s appearance surfaced after her two-night “Super Divas” concert with singer Regine Velasquez on Aug. 8 and 9.
Uploaded by previously fact-checked YouTube channel PHILIPPINES TRENDING NEWS, the untrue video has so far received 27,473 interactions.
VERA Files Fact Check has monitored several pieces of disinformation targeting Vice Ganda this month after she joked about former president Rodrigo Duterte’s 2016 campaign promise to travel via jetski to the West Philippine Sea and his current detainment in The Netherlands.
author: VERA Files
claim: Philippine senators cited Vice Ganda in contempt after she joked about Sen. Rodante Marcoleta during her concert.

title: Circulating news report promoting online gambling site ALTERED
content:
A video of broadcast journalist Pia Arcangel supposedly promoting an online gaming platform is circulating online. This is fake and may have been manipulated using artificial intelligence.
On Aug. 14, a Facebook page posted a one-minute and 26-second news report about the removal of ads for online gaming platforms. The report, bearing the logos of GMA Integrated News, SAKSI and PayMaya, then went on to promote the online gambling site JLJL88.
The video’s headline read:
“Content na nagpo-promote ng illegal online gambling, pinatatanggal ng CICC sa mga influencer (CICC wants influencers to remove content that promote illegal online gambling).”
From the 00:38-second mark of the reel’, Arcangel can be heard saying:
“Sa kasalukuyan, humigit-kumulang 270 lamang ang may opisyal na lisensya mula sa PAGCOR. Ngunit mayroon ding mga website na nagpapanggap at gumagamit ng pangalan ng mga lehitimong platform para linlangin ang publiko. Isa sa mga totoong legal at lisensyadong platform ay ang JLJL88. Nagbibigay ito ng malalaking premyo, madaling manalo ng jackpot at may garantiya ng ligtas at walang limitasyong withdrawal direkta sa inyong GCash account. Inirerekomenda ring sundin ang golden hours mula sa GCash upang mas tumaas ang inyong tyansa na manalo. Mula sa GMA Integrated News, ako po si JP Soriano. Para sa ligtas at legal na slot gaming, pumunta lamang sa JLJL88
(Currently, only about 270 have official licenses from PAGCOR. However, there are websites that pretend to be and use the names of legitimate platforms to deceive the public. One of the truly legal and licensed platforms is JLJL88. It offers big prizes, easy jackpot wins and guarantees safe unlimited withdrawals directly to your GCash account. It is also recommended to follow the golden hours from GCash to increase your chances of winning. From GMA Integrated News, I’m JP Soriano. For safe and legal slot gaming, just go to JLJL88).”
This is fake. The original GMA Integrated News did not promote any online gaming platform. A clone imitating Arcangel’s voice was added to deceive netizens.
GMA News’s official FB page uploaded the original video on July 12. The unedited report was about PAGCOR’s order to remove advertisements for online gambling. It never mentioned the site JLJL88.
JLJL88 is not on the list of PAGCOR-accredited gaming system administrators and registered domain names as of July 17.
Telltale signs of AI manipulation are obvious such as mouth movements that don’t match the audio with the speaker mispronouncing some Filipino words such as “kamakailan“, “patalastas,” “burahin,” “naglalaman” and “malalaking.“
The fake Arcangel also mistakenly introduced herself as “JP Soriano,” a male reporter of GMA Integrated News.
VERA Files tapped the Deepfakes Analysis Unit (DAU) of the India-based Misinformation Combat Alliance to verify if the video was AI-manipulated.
DAU ran the video in several AI detection tools. Hive AI audio classifier flagged the entire audio as “AI-generated.”
However, results from Hiya Audio Intelligence showed that the voice is authentic. “There is a 66% match with live human markers,” it concluded.
Further analysis using ElevenLabs speech classifier said the use of AI was “very unlikely.”
VERA Files has debunked other manipulated videos of journalists created to promote certain products.
The fake video circulated a day after the Senate Committee on Games and Amusement conducted a hearing on online gambling. According to Sen. Erwin Tulfo, majority of the 24 senators are in favor of a total ban.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. also said he has started organizing a conference among stakeholders – including the Catholic Bishops’ Conference of the Philippines, parents and regulators – to discuss the matter.
The post by the FB page ReelZone Club (created on June 5, 2025 as ALnahhas Clothes) garnered 1,200 reactions, 105 comments, 42 shares and 249,000 views.
author: VERA Files
claim: GMA Integrated News broadcast journalist Pia Arcangel endorsed an online gaming site in a report.

title: NO official order to scrap senior high school in 2026
content:
A photo posted on a Facebook page is claiming that senior high school under the K to 12 program will be scrapped in 2026, based on a supposed announcement from the Commission on Higher Education. This is fake.
The K to 12 program falls under the jurisdiction of the Department of Education, which has not released any such announcement. CHED also denied that the post originated from them.
Published on Aug. 6, the post features a graphic card with CHED’s logo to make it appear legitimate. It includes the following text:
“CHED Announcement
DAHIL bigo na MABIGYAN ng trabaho ang mga nakapag tapos ng K12, Wala ng K12 o Senior High sa 2026. Basahin sa Post ang details”
In the caption, the user also asked viewers to share the dubious post to help inform others.
In an email to VERA Files Fact Check, CHED confirmed that the post was not officially released by them and is ” deemed inauthentic.”
“Please also be informed that matters relating to K12 are under the jurisdiction of DepEd and not of our agency. We believe that the creator of this unofficial post does not know the difference between the two agencies and used the logo of the Commission in an attempt to make their post look authentic,” the agency said.
“Still, we highly encourage that citizens practice caution when consuming and believing such media as, again, these are not officially posted by the involved government agencies,” it added.
Prior to the start of the current school year, similar claims regarding the removal of Grade 11 and kindergarten also surfaced, which were already debunked by DepEd.
On June 2, Senator Jinggoy Estrada filed a bill scrapping senior high school for failing to deliver its goals. The bill is still pending on first reading in the Senate, according to the upper chamber’s website.
The fake post includes an affiliate link to an e-commerce site, a common tactic used by bogus pages to promote online products.
As of writing, the erroneous post, published by Facebook page Educational News and Updates (created Aug. 1) has so far garnered 526 total engagements.
author: VERA Files
claim: The Commission on Higher Education announced that senior high school under the K to 12 program will be abolished in 2026.

title: Trump will NOT get Duterte out of the ICC
content:
A YouTube video claims that U.S. President Donald Trump is angry and will take former president Rodrigo Duterte from the International Criminal Court detention facility in The Hague, Netherlands. This is false.
There are no credible reports confirming Trump’s involvement in the case of Duterte before the ICC. The video merely shows a clip that has no connection to the claim.
Published on Aug. 19, the 20-minute video features a vlog of former Duterte spokesperson Harry Roque in a split screen that also shows a man seemingly watching him. The clickbait thumbnail and title read:
“HALA! GALIT NA SI TRUMP KUNIN NA NIYA SI DUTERTE SA ICC.
“Geyera na! Galit na SI Trump,Kunin na nya SI Duterte.”
A quick search on Google does not show any news reports about the supposed move. The White House has also not released any statement about Trump taking action or even commenting on the case of Duterte, who has been arrested and detained for crimes against humanity in relation to this administration’s drug war campaign.
A reverse image search shows that the video came from a Facebook Live broadcast of Roque on Aug. 18. In the livestream, the former spokesperson discussed the recent summit in Alaska involving Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin.
At no point throughout the broadcast did Roque mention that Trump will take Duterte out of the ICC or that he supports the former president.
The false video appeared as the confirmation of charges hearing for Duterte approaches. The ICC Pre-Trial Chamber I has scheduled the proceedings to start on Sept. 23.
As of writing, the video posted by YouTube channel Viral BalitaPhilippines (created Feb. 4, 2022) has so far garnered 2,000 views, 199 likes, and 7 comments.
author: VERA Files
claim: U.S. President Donald Trump will get former president Rodrigo Duterte from the custody of the International Criminal Court.

title: FALSE claim that Rodrigo Duterte is free revived amid looming ICC trial
content:
Another YouTube video is erroneously spreading – again – the claim that former president Rodrigo Duterte has been released from his International Criminal Court detention cell in The Hague, Netherlands. This is not true.
On Aug. 18, a YouTube channel published the spurious video. Substituting some letters with numbers in an ostensible way to evade keyword detection, the video’s headline read:
“BIG GOODNEWS! LAHAT NA-SH0CKED? FPRRD NAKALABAS nasa 1CC? 100% UUWE sa P1NAS – KAUFMAN MARCOLETA
(Good news! Everyone was shocked? Former President Rodrigo Duterte is free from the ICC? Will now return to the Philippines 100% – Kaufman, Marcoleta).”
Aside from showing a thumbnail photo of Duterte and Vice President Sara Duterte waving their hands outside of an airplane door – implied to be taken after Duterte’s alleged release – the 21-minute video opened with the narrator saying that Duterte was no longer detained at the ICC facility.
VERA Files Fact Check has debunked this false claim numerous times, and Duterte remains in the ICC detention facility.
According to VERA Files Fact Check’s own reports, this claim first emerged weeks after the ICC arrested the former president in early March 2025. Since then, the falsehood keeps popping up every time there is an update on the status of Duterte’s ICC detention.
A reverse image search also revealed that the photo in the erroneous video’s thumbnail was taken years ago.
This fabricated claim continues to spread as the countdown nears for Duterte’s confirmation of charges hearing. The ICC Pre-Trial Chamber I provisionally scheduled the hearing on Sept. 23 to confirm the charges of crimes against humanity of murder, torture and rape.
YouTube channel PINAS NEWS INSIDER (created on Dec. 26, 2015) published the video, garnering over 12,400 online engagements and 186,000 views.
author: VERA Files
claim: Former president Rodrigo Duterte was released after being detained by the International Criminal Court for alleged crimes against humanity.

title: ABS-CBN did NOT ban Vice Ganda
content:
A Facebook post claims that ABS-CBN has banned comedian Vice Ganda from appearing in some of its programs after joking about former president Rodrigo Duterte which drew mixed reactions last week. This is false.
ABS-CBN has not banned Vice Ganda who remains a talent of ABS-CBN. As of Aug. 19, she continues to appear on the noontime variety show “It’s Showtime” where she has been a regular host since 2009.
Uploaded on Aug. 16, the erroneous post read:
“SH0CK NA BALITA! ABS-CBN, NAGDESISYON ng BAN kay Vice Ganda—Kontrobersya Laban sa Kanya, Ano ang Mangyayari sa Kanyang Karera?“
(Shocking news! ABS-CBN decided to ban Vice Ganda–controversy against her, what will happen to her career?)
There has been no announcement of a ban on Vice Ganda from the media company.
The comedian was absent from “It’s Showtime” for five days after getting the ire of Duterte supporters for poking fun at the ex-president’s campaign promise to go to the West Philippine Sea via jetski and his current detention at the Scheveningen Prison in The Netherlands during a concert skit last Aug. 9.
Vice Ganda returned to the program on Aug. 16 but has not publicly addressed the issue as of writing. She said her brief absence was to rest after her two-night concert with singer Regine Velasquez:
“I’m so happy to be back! Nakapagpahinga na ako. Humaba ‘yung tulog ko, nai-relax ko ‘yung legs ko (I am now well-rested. I slept longer and was able to relax my legs).”
VERA Files Fact Check earlier debunked disinformation on Vice Ganda supposedly being declared persona non grata in Davao City, the bailiwick of the Dutertes.
Uploaded by FB page Philippine Pie (created July 18, 2016 as Nagame), the post received 2,845 interactions.
author: VERA Files
claim: ABS-CBN has banned Vice Ganda after she made a joke involving former president Rodrigo Duterte.

title: Anne Curtis did NOT criticize Vice Ganda over Duterte joke
content:
Several Facebook posts are spreading an alleged quote from actress and television host Anne Curtis, criticizing comedian and her “It’s Showtime” co-host Vice Ganda for making fun of former President Rodrigo Duterte. The post is fake.
On Aug. 12, several FB pages and users published posts containing the fake post. Mimicking Curtis’ verified FB account, it had the following text:
“Sometimes, yung joke mo Vice hindi na talaga nakakatuwa, di mo talaga ramdam na nakakasakit kana (sic) ng tao, kahit yun man ang role mo mag-control ka naman sa mga biro mo, alam mo ba na iilan din kami na may lihim na galit sayo? Di ka nalang namin pinapatulan dahil lamang sa respito! (sic) Ang hilig mo magpahiya ng tao, di mo nga alam na ang andami na contestant na badtrip sayo!
(Sometimes, Vice, your jokes are really not funny anymore. You do not realize that you are already hurting people. Even if that is your role, control your jokes. Do you know how many of us are secretly angry at you? We just choose not to pick a fight with you out of respect! You really like embarrassing people, you do not even realize that so many contestants hate you!)”
Curtis denied making the statement and disowned the posts in her official X (formerly Twitter) account.
In an Aug. 12 tweet to invite fans to watch their noontime show, Curtis said these posts did not come from her, while poking fun at the ungrammatical writing style of the viral posts.
“And cguro naman by now alam nyo Hindi galing sa akin ung post that’s floating around about my sisterette!!! Konting RESPETO naman po (And maybe, by now, you know that the post that’s floating around about my sisterette did not come from me. A little respect, please),” she said.
The posts with the fake statement emerged after Vice Ganda, a close friend of Curtis, came under fire from Duterte supporters for poking fun at the former president’s 2016 remarks about jet-skiing to the West Philippine Sea. The comedian added that the skiing came with “water-bombing by Chinese vessels” and a “free trip to The Hague,” where Duterte is detained.
FB pages Thata Babaeng Bisaya (created on April 14, 2007), Arreza Brothers (Aug. 1, 2023), LJ TV (Jan. 8, 2025) and at least two other FB users spread the fake post, collectively garnering over 132,000 reactions, 53,700 comments and 9,000 shares.
author: VERA Files
claim: Anne Curtis criticized comedian Vice Ganda for making fun of former president Rodrigo Duterte.

title: Video of CHED-UniFAST scholarship application FAKE
content:
A video circulating on TikTok claims that the Unified Student Financial Assistance System for Tertiary Education (UniFAST), an attached agency of the Commission on Higher Education (CHED), is now accepting applications for its scholarship program. This is fake.
UniFAST has debunked this post.
Published on Aug.15, the erroneous video featured the logos of UniFAST and CHED to make it appear legitimate. Two narrators appeared in the video saying:
“May anak ka ba na nag-aaral sa high school at college? Ipalista mo na sa UniFAST scholarship. Nasa post ang registration link. 120,000 yearly allowance sa public school at 80,000 naman sa public plus 10,000 monthly allowance. Mag-apply na”
The caption of the post reads:
“CHED SCHOLARSHIP UNIFAST SCHOLARSHIP APPLICATION STARTS TODAY Apply now!”
In the comment section, the TikTok user who posted the video directs viewers to click the link provided in their profile bio to apply.
In an advisory posted on Facebook and TikTok on August 18, UniFAST labeled the post as “fake,” noting that it is misleading and may be part of a scam.
“UniFAST has not issued any official announcement regarding the Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) Program applications for Academic Year 2025–2026,” the advisory read.
“The Commission on Higher Education-UniFAST warns the public against unauthorized accounts misusing its name and logo to promote fake scholarship or financial aid application links, which often lead to phishing scams or spread of false information,” the agency added.
It also urged the public to check CHED’s official website or social media pages to verify announcements and to report accounts impersonating the agency.
The link in the user’s bio leads to a product listed on an online shopping platform.
Posted by TikTok user atty Harvey atayde, the post has so far garnered 1.8 million views, 43,400 shares, 37,200 reactions, and 5,676 comments.
author: VERA Files
claim: UniFAST announced that it is now accepting applications for its scholarship program.

title: Senate did NOT ‘junk’ VP Sara impeachment
content:
A Facebook page and a YouTube channel are claiming that the Senate has “junked” the Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte. This is not true.
After the hours-long debate during the Aug. 6 Senate session, 19 senators voted to archive the impeachment case against Duterte, following the immediately executory July 25 decision of the Supreme Court that declared the impeachment complaint unconstitutional.
First published by an FB page on Aug. 6, the post carried a caption that read:
“UPDATE|| Senado, ibinasura ang Articles of Impeachment laban kay VP Sara Duterte
Ibinasura ng Senado ang Articles of Impeachment laban kay Pangalawang Pangulo Sara Duterte sa botong 19 pabor, 4 tutol, at 1 abstain.
Sa ginanap na botohan ngayong araw, pinili ng mayorya ng mga senador na i-archive o itabi ang impeachment complaint—ibig sabihin, hindi na ito uusad pa sa susunod na mga pagdinig.
Hindi pa inilalabas ang buong detalye ng desisyon, ngunit ayon sa ilang mambabatas, wala umanong sapat na basehan ang reklamo.”
(UPDATE|| Senate junks Articles of Impeachment against VP Sara Duterte
The Senate has junked the Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte, with 19 senators voting in favor, 4 against, and 1 abstention.
In the vote held today, the majority of senators opted to archive the impeachment complaint—meaning it will not proceed in the future hearings.
The full details of the decision have yet to be released, but according to some lawmakers, the complaint allegedly lacked sufficient basis.)
The post also bore a graphic published by ABS-CBN News on Aug. 6, which clearly shows “how the senators voted on the motion to archive the impeachment case vs VP Sara Duterte.” While this post mentioned that the case was archived, it falsely claimed that it can no longer be re-opened.
A YouTube video uploaded on Aug. 7 also made a similar claim in its headline:
“SP Scudero Bumwelta kay Speaker Romualdez matapos Ibasura ang Impe4chment kay VP Sara Duterte Senado ([Senate President Francis “Chiz” Escudero] hits back at Speaker Romualdez after the Senate dismissed the impeachment against VP Sara Duterte)”
This is a mere clickbait. The video did not support its headline and showed clips of some senators’ speeches from the Aug. 6 session.
The Articles of Impeachment against the vice president were not junked. Although Sen. Rodante Marcoleta initially proposed a motion to dismiss Duterte’s impeachment, it was later amended to have the case archived.
In an interview published on Aug. 7, Senate Impeachment Court spokesperson Atty. Reginald Tongol told ABS-CBN News Channel that “there was no actual termination or dismissal of the Articles of Impeachment,” noting they were just referred to the archives.
Escudero said a motion to bring the impeachment case out of the archives could still be made “if the [SC] reverses itself, however possible or impossible that may be.”
On Aug. 5, the House filed a motion for reconsideration seeking a reversal of the High Court’s unanimous July 25 ruling. On the same day, the SC required Duterte, Davao-based lawyer Israeli Torreon, and other petitioners to comment on the motion within 10 days from notice.
The posts with untrue claims surfaced after the Senate archived the impeachment complaint against Duterte, with 19 votes in favor, four against, and one abstention.
Published by FB page Max Fm Tacurong City 91.1 Official (created on Jan. 25, 2020), the spurious post has so far received 428 interactions. The inaccurate video, uploaded by YouTube channel KABAYAN NEWS PH (April 29, 2020), has garnered 14,280 engagements as of writing.
author: VERA Files
claim: The Senate has junked the Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte.

title: AI-generated Taiwan flooding photos, MISLEADING
content:
Editor’s note: This fact check was produced with the help of a journalism student of the Polytechnic University of the Philippines as part of their internship at VERA Files.
Taiwan’s recent severe flooding has caused not only widespread concern internationally, but also triggered a wave of AI-generated photos exaggerating the extent of the calamity.
Facebook posts that circulated online from Aug. 5 to Aug. 7, contained hyperbolic and AI-generated photos misrepresenting it as news content. Its headline read:
“BREAKING NEWS — TAIWAN
Massive Floods Devastate City: Roads Collapse, Buildings Tilt as Emergency Crews Rush to Respond
Taipei, Taiwan — August 4, 2025″
This is a clickbait. The original post redirects netizens to a YouTube link containing a conspiracy video, while other similar posts were used to promote their subscription channels.
Typhoon Danas thrashed southern Taiwan for a month with unparalleled winds, causing four people to die, hundreds to be injured, widespread power outages, and thousands to be evacuated. Photos used in the bogus post exaggerated the depiction of the calamity.
Contrary to what the photos showed, no buildings tilted, neither were there any highway sections torn apart, nor any collapsed bridges due to the heavy and nonstop rainfall that occurred in Taiwan as part of the devastation of Typhoon Danas.
VERA Files ran the photos through the AI-detection tool Hive moderation and found them to have 99.9% likelihood of AI-manipulation.
This was posted by an FB page “EndTimes News” that is claimed to be a personal blog of some digital creator who features fearmongering and clickbait content presented as some sort of ‘prophecy’.
VERA Files has flagged a number of misleading posts on typhoon similar to this.
The misleading posts have so far collectively garnered a total of 37,923 reactions; 4,619 comments; and 7,518 shares.
author: VERA Files
claim: Photos show severe flooding in Taiwan in July 2025.

title: Govt NOT stopping 4Ps
content:
Circulating on Facebook is a graphic of a supposed announcement that the government is stopping the Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program or 4Ps with the final payout supposedly in August. This is fake.
The Department of Social Welfare and Development made no such announcement. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said in his 2025 State of the Nation Address that the conditional cash transfer program for poor families will continue.
Posted on July 19 but is continuously being shared, the bogus graphic read:
“ANNOUNCEMENT. Pabatid sa lahat ng 4Ps ito ay tatanggalin na ng gobyerno. 4PS TANGGAL NA. Huling payout sa August 2025.”
(Attention to all beneficiaries, the 4Ps will be removed by the government. 4Ps removed. Final payout in August 2025).
The 4Ps conditional cash transfer program is institutionalized under Republic Act 11310. It cannot be abolished without Congress passing another law to repeal it.
In his 2025 SONA, Marcos urged lawmakers to amend the 4Ps law to ensure that beneficiaries have improved their living conditions first before exiting the program.
Under the current law, 4Ps beneficiaries are required to exit the program after seven years. The DSWD said the president wants for beneficiaries’ life quality to be assessed first before they leave the program and stop receiving financial assistance.
Households under the 4Ps program receive a P750 health grant and P600 rice subsidy every month. A maximum of three children per family get an education grant for 10 months every year depending on their grade level: P300 for elementary, P500 for junior high school and P700 for senior high school. Pregnant mothers are entitled to a P350 monthly allowance for a year.
This post continues to be shared as Sen. Erwin Tulfo said in his Aug. 3 statement that he will propose replacing the 4Ps with a livelihood capital program for poor families. But no actual measure has been filed yet.
Uploaded by three FB users, the post received over a thousand shares.
author: VERA Files
claim: The government is stopping the Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program or 4Ps

title: NO naval standoff between PH and Malaysia near Sabah
content:
A video posted by a YouTube channel claims that Philippine and Malaysian naval vessels were recently involved in a tense standoff near Sabah. This is fake.
There are no credible reports confirming that such an incident took place. Both countries have said the claim is fabricated.
Published on Aug. 12, the 12-minute 51-second video opened with a narration describing the supposed incident. It alleged that Philippine Navy vessel BRP Magat Salamat approached fishing boats in the Sulu Sea, prompting the Malaysian patrol ship KD Pahang to issue a loud warning ordering the Philippine vessel to leave.
The video further claimed that the tension escalated when the Filipino ship refused to retreat. An anonymous speaker stated:
“For hours, the two warships floated just hundreds of meters apart. Their guns uncovered and crews at battle stations, every sailor tense and alert. The silence was heavy, broken only by the sound of the sea and the occasional crackle of radio static.”
“One wrong move, one misunderstood signal could spark a conflict with consequences far beyond these waters. This was August 11th, 2025.”
The narrator claimed that the incident ended without violence after negotiations and back-channel communications.
In a statement posted on Facebook last Aug.12, the Armed Forces of the Philippines debunked the claim and labelled it as “fake.” It said:
“This is entirely fabricated—no such incident occurred. It intends to strain our 60-year diplomatic ties with Malaysia, erode public trust in the AFP, and provoke unnecessary tensions.”
The AFP added that such disinformation threatened peace and stability in the region and served only political or strategic agendas.
On Aug. 14, the Royal Malaysian Navy, the naval arm of the Malaysian Armed Forces, also dismissed the claim as false and noted that “no incident as depicted in the video took place.”
Adding to its dubiousness, the YouTube video also used no actual footage of the “standoff” and instead featured a compilation of AI-generated clips, stock imagery, footage of animals, and flashes of unrelated news reports about sports.
In the description box, the channel disclosed that its video contained “altered or synthetic content” and that it “was created with the assistance of artificial intelligence combined with human research, analysis, and editorial oversight to ensure accuracy, depth, and clarity.”
Posted by YouTube channel INFO SPARK HUB (created June 13), the fake video has garnered 2,400 views and 77 comments, and 15 likes.
author: VERA Files
claim: Philippine and Malaysian navy ships had a standoff near Sabah on Aug. 11, 2025.

title: Vice Ganda DID NOT bid goodbye to It’s Showtime due to ‘jetski’ joke
content:
Two videos circulating on Facebook and TikTok claim to show comedian Vice Ganda bidding farewell to her co-hosts in the noontime show “It’s Showtime”, allegedly after joking about former president Rodrigo Duterte in a recent concert. This is false.
The clips are from the 2023 final episode of the noontime show on TV5, where Vice Ganda thanked the station, before they moved to GTV.
Published on Aug. 14 on TikTok, the two-minute clip shows the comedian giving messages to her fellow hosts one by one. At the start of the misleading video, Vice Ganda can be heard saying:
“Ang aking mga kapwa Showtime hosts, maraming maraming salamat sa pagkapit ninyo. Alam ko pagod na rin kayo. Pagod na rin ako pero di ko yun ininda, kasi kumakapit rin naman tayo sa isa’t-isa”
The video also contains overlaid text and a caption that reads:
“Vice Ganda emosyonal na nagpapaalam sa Showtime family dahil sa Vice Ganda concert issue.”
Part of the video was shared by a user on FB reels on Aug. 16, with a nearly identical caption about the supposed emotional farewell.
A reverse image search using keyframes from the video led to a YouTube video uploaded by ABS-CBN’s official channel on June 30, 2023. It was the daily show’s final airing on TV5 before the program moved to GTV, a sister-channel of GMA-7.
The TikTok clip was trimmed from the start of the original video up to the 3:13 timestamp, while the Facebook reel was taken from the 3:59 mark.
Another portion of the same episode was posted by ABS-CBN on the same day with the title: “Vice Ganda expresses his gratitude to TV5”. The description box also states: “Vice Ganda expresses his gratitude to TV5 for taking care of “It’s Showtime.”
Although the comedian skipped a few episodes early last week to take a break after her concert with singer Regine Velasquez, she returned to the show on Aug. 16.
The misleading clips surfaced after Vice Ganda became controversial for a joke referencing Duterte’s failed promise to ride a jetski to the West Philippine Sea, and his detention at The Hague, Netherlands.
Posted by TikTok user gomezvincent08, the video has garnered 871,000 views, 14,600 likes, 1,608 comments, and 1,015 shares. The one posted by Facebook user Lhen Andaya has garnered 3.5 million views, 3,200 comments, and 487 shares.
author: VERA Files
claim: Vice Ganda bid goodbye to her “It’s Showtime” co-hosts due to the controversial joke about former president Rodrigo Duterte in her concert.

title: Circulating graphic on ‘P3,000 fee for voter registration’ FAKE
content:
A graphic claiming that the Commission on Elections has started charging P3,000 for voter registration is circulating online. This is fake and came from an impostor page.
On Aug. 12, an FB page posted a social media card with the Comelec’s logo and text that read:
“Announcement. Maniningil na ang Comelec ng 3,000 Pesos sa lahat ng mag papa rehistro. Walk in simula August 15, 2025. MAY BAYAD NA ANG MAG PA REHISTRO (Comelec will now charge 3,000 Pesos register. Walk in starting August 15, 2025. REGISTRATION NOW HAS A FEE).”
This is not true. The Comelec did not release such a graphic and voter registration remains free of charge.
In an Aug. 15 Viber message to VERA Files, Comelec Spokesperson Atty. John Rex Laudiangco confirmed the graphic is fake and from an impostor page.
The Comelec’s official FB page has since posted an advisory debunking the fake announcement and clarifying that voter registration is a constitutional right for qualified Filipinos and there is no payment for it.
The Commission also warned netizens of the impostor page Comelec Ph. It advised netizens to get official updates only from Comelec’s official website, comelec.gov.ph, and social media pages.
Voter registration requirements and procedures are also listed on Comelec’s website.
The link attached to the fake post leads to an e-commerce site. This is a form of affiliate marketing that uses disinformation to drive users to a product site.
The graphic circulated a day after the Comelec announced that it had processed almost 2.8 million applicants during the voter registration period for the barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan elections on Aug. 1 to 10.
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has since postponed the BSKE from December 2025 to November 2026.
VERA Files has debunked other fabricated announcements attributed to Comelec.
The post by the impostor FB page Comelec Ph, which was created only on Aug. 11, garnered 165 interactions. The graphic has been shared on other FB groups, gaining additional traction.
author: VERA Files
claim: The Commission on Election has started charging P3,000 for voter registration since August 15, 2025.

title: Hontiveros errs on reversal of ‘unanimous’ SC rulings
content:
During the interpellation on the fate of the impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte, Sen. Risa Hontiveros cited two instances where the Supreme Court allegedly reversed its unanimous decision. One case involved the League of Cities of the Philippines v. Commission on Elections and the other, the International Service for the Acquisition of Agri-Biotech Applications, Inc. (ISAAA) v. Greenpeace Southeast Asia.
Hontiveros was wrong. None of the six decisions—including reversals—in the League of Cities v. Comelec case was decided unanimously. The senator has since issued a correction.
During the Aug. 6 plenary session, Sen. Rodante Marcoleta moved to dismiss the impeachment case following the SC decision declaring it unconstitutional.
